description: Cross-framework mapping from CSDDD due diligence obligations to GRI 308 (Supplier Environmental Assessment) and GRI 414 (Supplier Social Assessment)
sourceFrameworkName: EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (2024/1760)
targetFrameworkName: GRI Standards 2021 — GRI 308 (Supplier Environmental Assessment) and GRI 414 (Supplier Social Assessment)
notes: Mapping between CSDDD due diligence process obligations and GRI 308/414 supplier assessment disclosures. GRI 308 and 414 focus on disclosure of supplier screening and assessment practices; CSDDD mandates the underlying due diligence processes. Companies reporting under GRI and subject to CSDDD can use their CSDDD due diligence outputs to fulfil GRI 308/414 disclosures.

mappings.source_name: Integrating due diligence into policies and management systems
mappings.target_name: New suppliers that were screened using environmental criteria
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 7 policy integration provides the policy framework under which environmental supplier screening (GRI 308-1) would be conducted. CSDDD is broader (all adverse impacts) while GRI 308-1 is specifically about percentage of new suppliers screened.

mappings.source_name: Integrating due diligence into policies and management systems
mappings.target_name: New suppliers that were screened using social criteria
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 7 policy integration provides the policy framework under which social supplier screening (GRI 414-1) would be conducted. CSDDD is broader while GRI 414-1 focuses on percentage of new suppliers screened using social criteria.

mappings.source_name: Identifying and assessing actual and potential adverse impacts
mappings.target_name: Negative environmental impacts in the supply chain and actions taken
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 8 identification of environmental adverse impacts in the value chain directly supports GRI 308-2 disclosure of negative environmental impacts in the supply chain. CSDDD adds the obligation to consult stakeholders.

mappings.source_name: Identifying and assessing actual and potential adverse impacts
mappings.target_name: Negative social impacts in the supply chain and actions taken
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 8 identification of human rights adverse impacts directly supports GRI 414-2 disclosure of negative social impacts in the supply chain.

mappings.source_name: Preventing and mitigating potential adverse impacts
mappings.target_name: Negative environmental impacts in the supply chain and actions taken
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 10-11 prevention and mitigation measures map to GRI 308-2's requirement to disclose actions taken to address identified environmental impacts, including contractual assurances and capacity building.

mappings.source_name: Preventing and mitigating potential adverse impacts
mappings.target_name: Negative social impacts in the supply chain and actions taken
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 10-11 prevention of human rights impacts maps to GRI 414-2's requirement to disclose actions taken including number of suppliers where improvements were agreed upon or relationships were terminated.

mappings.source_name: Bringing actual adverse impacts to an end
mappings.target_name: Negative environmental impacts in the supply chain and actions taken
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 12 remediation of actual environmental impacts aligns with GRI 308-2 disclosure of actions taken. GRI 308-2 requests number of suppliers assessed, identified as having impacts, and significant impacts identified.

mappings.source_name: Bringing actual adverse impacts to an end
mappings.target_name: Negative social impacts in the supply chain and actions taken
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 12 remediation of actual social/human rights impacts aligns with GRI 414-2. Both require actions including improvements agreed, relationships terminated as last resort.

mappings.source_name: Establishing and maintaining a complaints procedure
mappings.target_name: Negative social impacts in the supply chain and actions taken
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 14 complaints procedure supports the grievance mechanisms referenced in GRI 414-2. GRI 414-2 does not explicitly require a complaints mechanism but asks about actions taken, which would include grievance handling.

mappings.source_name: Monitoring the effectiveness of due diligence
mappings.target_name: Negative environmental impacts in the supply chain and actions taken
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 15 monitoring effectiveness feeds into GRI 308-2's quantitative disclosures (number of suppliers assessed, improvements agreed, relationships terminated). Monitoring provides the data for GRI reporting.

mappings.source_name: Monitoring the effectiveness of due diligence
mappings.target_name: Negative social impacts in the supply chain and actions taken
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 15 monitoring effectiveness feeds into GRI 414-2's quantitative disclosures. GRI 414-2 requests metrics on supplier assessments, identified impacts, and improvement actions.

mappings.source_name: Integrating due diligence into policies — code of conduct
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 7 code of conduct for employees and subsidiaries relates to GRI 2-23 policy commitments disclosure. GRI 2-23 is a general disclosure standard, not specific to supply chain, but covers the same policy commitment territory.

mappings.source_name: Identifying and assessing actual and potential adverse impacts — stakeholder consultation
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 8 requires consulting affected stakeholders during impact identification. This relates to GRI 2-29's disclosure on approach to stakeholder engagement, though GRI 2-29 is a general disclosure.
